# goleak

```go
import "go.uber.org/goleak"
```

Package goleak is a Goroutine leak detector.

## Index

- [func Find\(options ...Option\) error](<#Find>)
- [func VerifyNone\(t TestingT, options ...Option\)](<#VerifyNone>)
- [func VerifyTestMain\(m TestingM, options ...Option\)](<#VerifyTestMain>)
- [type Option](<#Option>)
  - [func Cleanup\(cleanupFunc func\(exitCode int\)\) Option](<#Cleanup>)
  - [func IgnoreAnyFunction\(f string\) Option](<#IgnoreAnyFunction>)
  - [func IgnoreCreatedBy\(f string\) Option](<#IgnoreCreatedBy>)
  - [func IgnoreCurrent\(\) Option](<#IgnoreCurrent>)
  - [func IgnoreTopFunction\(f string\) Option](<#IgnoreTopFunction>)
  - [func RunOnFailure\(\) Option](<#RunOnFailure>)
- [type TestingM](<#TestingM>)
- [type TestingT](<#TestingT>)

<a name="Find"></a>

## func [Find](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/leaks.go#L55>)

```go
func Find(options ...Option) error
```

Find looks for extra goroutines, and returns a descriptive error if any are found.

<a name="VerifyNone"></a>

## func [VerifyNone](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/leaks.go#L94>)

```go
func VerifyNone(t TestingT, options ...Option)
```

VerifyNone marks the given TestingT as failed if any extra goroutines are found by Find. This is a helper method to make it easier to integrate in tests by doing:

```
defer VerifyNone(t)
```

VerifyNone is currently incompatible with t.Parallel because it cannot associate specific goroutines with specific tests. Thus, non\-leaking goroutines from other tests running in parallel could fail this check. If you need to run tests in parallel, use [VerifyTestMain](<#VerifyTestMain>) instead, which will verify that no leaking goroutines exist after ALL tests finish.

<a name="VerifyTestMain"></a>

## func [VerifyTestMain](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/testmain.go#L52>)

```go
func VerifyTestMain(m TestingM, options ...Option)
```

VerifyTestMain can be used in a TestMain function for package tests to verify that there were no goroutine leaks. To use it, your TestMain function should look like:

```
func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
  goleak.VerifyTestMain(m)
}
```

See https://pkg.go.dev/testing#hdr-Main for more details.

This will run all tests as per normal, and if they were successful, look for any goroutine leaks and fail the tests if any leaks were found.

<a name="Option"></a>

## type [Option](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/options.go#L31-L33>)

Option lets users specify custom verifications.

```go
type Option interface {
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="Cleanup"></a>

### func [Cleanup](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/options.go#L103>)

```go
func Cleanup(cleanupFunc func(exitCode int)) Option
```

Cleanup sets up a cleanup function that will be executed at the end of the leak check. When passed to [VerifyTestMain](<#VerifyTestMain>), the exit code passed to cleanupFunc will be set to the exit code of TestMain. When passed to [VerifyNone](<#VerifyNone>), the exit code will be set to 0. This cannot be passed to [Find](<#Find>).

<a name="IgnoreAnyFunction"></a>

### func [IgnoreAnyFunction](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/options.go#L82>)

```go
func IgnoreAnyFunction(f string) Option
```

IgnoreAnyFunction ignores goroutines where the specified function is present anywhere in the stack.

The function name must be fully qualified, e.g.,

```
go.uber.org/goleak.IgnoreAnyFunction
```

For methods, the fully qualified form looks like:

```
go.uber.org/goleak.(*MyType).MyMethod
```

<a name="IgnoreCreatedBy"></a>

### func [IgnoreCreatedBy](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/options.go#L91>)

```go
func IgnoreCreatedBy(f string) Option
```

IgnoreCreatedBy ignores any goroutines that where spawned from the specified function. The function name should be fully qualified, e.g. go.uber.org/goleak.IgnoreCreatedBy.

<a name="IgnoreCurrent"></a>

### func [IgnoreCurrent](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/options.go#L111>)

```go
func IgnoreCurrent() Option
```

IgnoreCurrent records all current goroutines when the option is created, and ignores them in any future Find/Verify calls.

<a name="IgnoreTopFunction"></a>

### func [IgnoreTopFunction](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/options.go#L66>)

```go
func IgnoreTopFunction(f string) Option
```

IgnoreTopFunction ignores any goroutines where the specified function is at the top of the stack. The function name should be fully qualified, e.g., go.uber.org/goleak.IgnoreTopFunction

<a name="RunOnFailure"></a>

### func [RunOnFailure](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/options.go#L123>)

```go
func RunOnFailure() Option
```

RunOnFailure makes goleak look for leaking goroutines upon test failures. By default goleak only looks for leaking goroutines when tests succeed.

<a name="TestingM"></a>

## type [TestingM](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/testmain.go#L36-L38>)

TestingM is the minimal subset of testing.M that we use.

```go
type TestingM interface {
    Run() int
}
```

<a name="TestingT"></a>

## type [TestingT](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/leaks.go#L31-L33>)

TestingT is the minimal subset of testing.TB that we use.

```go
type TestingT interface {
    Error(...interface{})
}
```

# stack

```go
import "go.uber.org/goleak/internal/stack"
```

Package stack is used for parsing stacks from \`runtime.Stack\`.

<a name="Stack"></a>

## type [Stack](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/internal/stack/stacks.go#L36-L51>)

Stack represents a single Goroutine's stack.

```go
type Stack struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="All"></a>

### func [All](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/internal/stack/stacks.go#L246>)

```go
func All() []Stack
```

All returns the stacks for all running goroutines.

<a name="Current"></a>

### func [Current](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/internal/stack/stacks.go#L251>)

```go
func Current() Stack
```

Current returns the stack for the current goroutine.

<a name="Stack.CreatedBy"></a>

### func \(Stack\) [CreatedBy](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/internal/stack/stacks.go#L69>)

```go
func (s Stack) CreatedBy() string
```

CreatedBy returns the name of the function that spawned the goroutine.

<a name="Stack.FirstFunction"></a>

### func \(Stack\) [FirstFunction](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/internal/stack/stacks.go#L74>)

```go
func (s Stack) FirstFunction() string
```

FirstFunction returns the name of the first function on the stack.

<a name="Stack.Full"></a>

### func \(Stack\) [Full](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/internal/stack/stacks.go#L64>)

```go
func (s Stack) Full() string
```

Full returns the full stack trace for this goroutine.

<a name="Stack.HasFunction"></a>

### func \(Stack\) [HasFunction](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/internal/stack/stacks.go#L80>)

```go
func (s Stack) HasFunction(name string) bool
```

HasFunction reports whether the stack has the given function anywhere in it.

<a name="Stack.ID"></a>

### func \(Stack\) [ID](<https://github.com/uber-go/goleak/blob/master/internal/stack/stacks.go#L54>)

```go
func (s Stack) ID() int
```

ID returns the goroutine ID.

func (s Stack) State() string
